channel.title: Sentence Correction on Arnesh's Website
channel.description: Recent content in Sentence Correction on Arnesh's Website

item.title: SC-4 Modifiers
item.description: A modifier describes/provides extra information about something else in the sentence. Incorrectly used modifiers lead to ambiguity or illogical meaning. 💡 Many modifiers answer the questions who, what, when, where, or why Adjectives & Adverbs These are one-word modifiers. Adjective → ONLY Noun/Pronoun Adverb → Almost anything EXCEPT Noun/Pronoun Verbs, Adjectives Prepositional Phrases Entire Clauses Many adverbs are formed by adding “-ly” to the adjective 💡 Often Adjective ↔ Adverb use will be interchanged as the sentence error.

item.title: SC-3 Sentence Structure
item.description: Every sentence must have a noun (subject) that performs the action (verb). Subject-Verb Errors Subject/Verb Missing A sentence missing the subject/verb is just a fragment. In GMAT, the correct answer must be a complete sentence or independent clause. E.g: The cat sitting by the stairs. ❌ 🚨 Verbs ≠ Complete Sentence Here although sitting looks like a verb, its a working verb that can run a sentence by itself. The cat sitting by the stairs WATCHED the mouse ✅ | watched is a working verb here.

item.title: SC-2 Grammar and Meaning
item.description: Must-Know Grammar Terms Clause Set of words containing a subject and a working verb Eg: She applied for the job. She- subject & applied- working verb 💡 Using the question “Who did what?” will point to the subject and working verb Independent Clause: Standalone correct sentence. Dependent Clause: Also contains a verb. But not standalone in a sentence. These are your sentence fragments. Modifier Provides additional information in a sentence. Adjectives are a modifier.

item.title: SC-1 Question Format and Solution Process
item.description: Question Format Although William Pereira first gained national recognition for his movie set designs, including those for Reap the Wild Wind and Jane Eyre, future generations remember him as the architect of the Transamerica Tower, the Malibu campus of Pepperdine University, and the city of Irvine. (A) including those for Reap the Wild Wind and Jane Eyre, future generations remember (B) like that for Reap the Wild Wind and Jane Eyre, future generations will remember
